unit.note: Full suites re-run in the iterate worktree. server: 193 files, 2253 passed + 1 skipped (includes the new ci-action-pinning-posture.test.ts, 8 cases — 6 original plus 2 added after the Tier-3 reviewer found the quoted-ref hole). client: 295 files, 2695 passed. 0 failures; tsc --noEmit clean both workspaces; oxlint exit 0 with no new warnings.

integration.note: webui vitest does not separate an integration layer; covered within the unit suites.

e2e.note: No product UI/API surface changed — the diff is CI workflow definitions, a deleted Dependabot config, a compliance register and one vitest. The end-to-end proof for THIS change is this PR's own CI run executing the restored mutable action tags (GitHub refuses to run invalid workflow YAML, so a green run proves they resolve and execute).

smoke.note: No server/app started; a CI-configuration change has no smoke surface.

surface_verification.justification: No startable application surface: the diff changes GitHub Actions workflow definitions, deletes a Dependabot config, adds a compliance register file and one vitest. No route, component, CLI entry point or server behavior is touched. The two surfaces that DO exist were driven directly: (1) the workflow definitions are executed by this PR's own CI run; (2) the .trivyignore.yaml register was verified against the SHIPPED consumer parse_accepted_risks() imported by path from the compliance plugin, asserting expired=False today and expired=True at now=2027-07-19 - the re-review alarm is proven to ring by injecting a future date rather than waiting for one.

behaviors.behavior: The posture guard itself cannot silently pass on a QUOTED ref (regression found in Tier-3 review of this file's first version)
behaviors.evidence: ci-action-pinning-posture.test.ts 'returns refs without surrounding quotes' + 'classifies a QUOTED SHA-pinned github-owned ref as a violation' PASSED. With the quote attached, `uses: 'actions/checkout@<sha>'` fails BOTH the owner-prefix and the trailing-SHA check, so a quoted pin would sail through every assertion while looking guarded. Exercised against a synthetic fixture, not only the real files, so the hole stays closed even though no workflow quotes its refs today

behaviors.behavior: 8 first-party actions/* + github/* refs restored to mutable tags (AC-1)
behaviors.evidence: ci-action-pinning-posture.test.ts 'pins no actions/* or github/* reference to a commit SHA' PASSED; independently, git diff 789dbec2~1 -- .github/workflows is empty (byte-exact pre-#285 state)
ci-action-pinning-posture.test.ts 'pins EVERY third-party reference to a full 40-char commit SHA' PASSED (asserted positively, so a too-greedy unpin fails loudly)
ci-action-pinning-posture.test.ts 'has no .github/dependabot.yml' — RED before the change, PASSED after

behaviors.behavior: All 6 restored workflows remain valid YAML
behaviors.evidence: yaml.safe_load pre-flight over .github/workflows/*.yml -> ALL VALID, 12 jobs. No committed regression test: neither workspace has a YAML parser and GitHub rejects invalid workflow YAML anyway (see behavior 6)

behaviors.behavior: Workflows still execute correctly under mutable tags
behaviors.evidence: this PR's own CI run — all jobs green except the expected 'PR Review' policy failure on any .github/workflows/* change
probe against the SHIPPED consumer: parse_accepted_risks(root, now=2026-07-18) -> 1 row, id semgrep:github-actions-mutable-action-tag, expired_at parsed as a date, expired=False

behaviors.behavior: The re-review alarm actually fires once past expired_at
behaviors.evidence: same consumer with now=2027-07-19 -> expired=True; boundary probe confirms it expires strictly AFTER the date. Verified by injecting a future now, not by waiting

behaviors.behavior: ADR records the posture, asymmetry and supersession (AC-5)
behaviors.reason: prose adequacy is a review judgment; presence and field structure are covered by the F11 ADR checks

GitHub alert state, and the alerts cannot exist until main carries the revert — post-merge API read-back is the evidence. Pre-merge inventory already measured: Semgrep 1 (self-resolving), Scorecard 35 (orphaned, out of scope)
GitHub PR state; post-merge API read-back. Dependabot may close it itself once its config is gone

behaviors.behavior: Monorepo triage item filed for the framework follow-up (AC-10)
behaviors.evidence: PASS — triage.read_all_items(C:/01_Development/shipwright) returns exactly 1 item with dedupKey=ci-supplychain-guardrails-and-acceptance-surfacing: trg-9509c2e8, status=triage, severity=medium, kind=improvement, launchPayload present. The read-back earned its place: the first attempt reported FAIL because the append API takes dedup_key (snake_case) while read_all_items normalizes to dedupKey (camelCase) — a bug in the assertion, not the write. It also revealed the item routes to the TRACKED triage.jsonl here, not the gitignored outbox
